drafts.subject: Protocol review request for an agent benchmark
drafts.body:
Hi METR team,

I am building OrgX-Bench, a benchmark for autonomous initiative work: long-horizon, tool-using workplace tasks with hidden validators, loss accounting, human baselines, and outside reproduction.

I am reaching out because METR's time-horizon work is one of the references shaping our protocol. The ask is a focused 30-minute critique of the human-baseline and release-gate design before we publish any headline claims.

We already have strict validators for model/run manifests, private holdout commitments, human-baseline coverage, third-party replication evidence, and public reproduction receipts. I want the protocol to be hard to dismiss before the frontier sweep runs.

Would you be open to reviewing the protocol or pointing me to the right person for critique?
drafts.markdown:
# methodology-metr

Lane: methodology_advisor
Status: drafted
Priority: P0
Contact: public_email: info@metr.org
Send Ready: yes
Dispatch Ready Now: no
Action Type: send_email
Planned Window: future
Block Reasons: none
Evidence Gaps: timed-human-baselines, frontier-headline-release
Ask: 30-minute human-baseline and time-horizon methodology critique.
Rationale: METR is the strongest methodological reference for human-expert task duration, reliability curves, and time-horizon framing.

Subject: Protocol review request for an agent benchmark

drafts.name: Sierra Research team
drafts.ask: Small independent sealed-world replication attempt or referral to the right evaluator.
drafts.subject: Independent replication request for an agent benchmark
drafts.body:
Hi Sierra Research team,

I am preparing an external validation pass for OrgX-Bench, a benchmark for autonomous initiative work with sealed holdout validators and strict loss accounting.

The ask is not endorsement. It is an independent replication attempt on two sealed holdout worlds through the submission API, with a signed result or a published discrepancy log.

The goal is to make the release checkable by someone outside OrgX before any headline claims are made.

Would your team be open to a small replication run, or is there someone you would recommend for this?

Rationale: tau-bench is directly relevant to stateful tool-agent-user interaction, final-state grading, and pass^k reliability.

drafts.organization: Paid SRE / incident response practitioner pool
drafts.contact_value: Respondent, User Interviews, Prolific, or CloudResearch Connect
drafts.ask: Recruit at least six paid operators covering incident_response, resilience_ops, deploy_governance, and support_sla.
block_reasons: target is a pool/marketplace listing, not a direct recipient.
drafts.subject: Paid timed expert session for an agent benchmark
drafts.body:
Hi there,

I am recruiting paid domain practitioners for timed baseline sessions in OrgX-Bench, a benchmark for autonomous initiative work.

The ask is a 45-90 minute timed task using provided instructions and allowed tools only. You would produce the work artifact, record timing, and consent to hashed non-identifying aggregate reporting. You will not see private validators or answers.

This is not a marketing quote. It is baseline data so model results are compared against real human work.

Would you be open to participating or referring a qualified incident response, resilience ops, deploy governance, and support sla operator?

drafts.ask: Fixed-fee public repo reproduction once a release candidate exists.
block_reasons: target needs a warm intro before direct outreach.
drafts.subject: Outside reproduction request for a benchmark release
drafts.body:
Hi there,

I am looking for an outside reviewer to reproduce a public benchmark release from files only.

The ask is to pull the public repo, run the documented reproduction command, record input hashes, command output, result hash, deviations, and whether the public headline numbers match to the digit.

This is a reproducibility check, not an endorsement.

Would you be open to doing this for a fixed review fee once the release candidate is ready?
drafts.markdown:
# reproduction-independent-engineer

Lane: stranger_reproduction_reviewer
Status: drafted
Priority: P0
Contact: warm_intro_needed
Send Ready: no
Dispatch Ready Now: no
Action Type: request_warm_intro
Planned Window: future
Block Reasons: target needs a warm intro before direct outreach.
Evidence Gaps: stranger-reproduction
Ask: Fixed-fee public repo reproduction once a release candidate exists.
Rationale: The public release needs a non-OrgX reviewer who can run the reproduction command and sign the receipt without being involved in benchmark construction.

Subject: Outside reproduction request for a benchmark release

Hi there,

I am looking for an outside reviewer to reproduce a public benchmark release from files only.

The ask is to pull the public repo, run the documented reproduction command, record input hashes, command output, result hash, deviations, and whether the public headline numbers match to the digit.

This is a reproducibility check, not an endorsement.

Would you be open to doing this for a fixed review fee once the release candidate is ready?
